title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: Soil spectral modeling, visualization, prediction — powered Open Soil Spectral Library (OSSL v1.2) soilVAE asymmetric autoencoders. Two sensor-agnostic models cover complete VisNIR MIR spectral ranges data field laboratory instrument.

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: Overview Models Sensor-Agnostic Methodology Supported Instruments Performance Benchmarks Architecture Preprocessing Pipeline Soil Properties OSSL Data Sources Installation Quick Start Shiny Interface Versioning Data Citations Software Citation License

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: autoSpectra R package interactive Shiny application predicting soil physical chemical properties diffuse reflectance spectra. provides two official OSSL-trained models accept spectra VisNIR MIR instrument without modification: models trained full Open Soil Spectral Library v1.2, largest publicly available, multi-instrument, multi-continent soil spectral archive. core predictive engine soilVAE — asymmetric autoencoder simultaneously reconstructs spectrum predicts soil properties shared 16-dimensional latent space.

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
code: Family ID  : OSSL_VisNIR Domain     : Visible + Near-Infrared reflectance Grid       : 350 – 2500 nm at 2 nm (1 076 bands) Pipeline   : −ln(R) → SG smooth(11,2) → SG 1st deriv(11,2,1) Training   : OSSL v1.2 L0 spectra + L1 soil lab (all contributing datasets) Validation : 10-fold CV stratified by OSSL contributing dataset Properties : 34 OSSL L1 harmonized soil variables

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
code: Family ID  : OSSL_MIR Domain     : Mid-Infrared absorbance (ATR, DRIFTS) Grid       : 600 – 4000 cm⁻¹ at 2 cm⁻¹ (1 701 bands) Pipeline   : SG smooth(11,2) → SG 1st deriv(11,2,1) Training   : OSSL v1.2 L0 spectra + L1 soil lab (all contributing datasets) Validation : 10-fold CV stratified by OSSL contributing dataset Properties : 34 OSSL L1 harmonized soil variables

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: sensor-agnostic design achieved restricting models common spectral band overlap. result four mutually reinforcing scientific decisions:

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: OSSL v1.2 aggregates contributions 30+ independent datasets collected across multiple continents diverse instruments (ASD, Foss, Bruker, PerkinElmer, others). Training corpus forces model generalise beyond single instrument’s characteristics.

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: canonical preprocessing pipeline eliminates two dominant sources inter-instrument spectral variation: two-step approach — smooth first, differentiate second — gives cleaner noise removal single combined SG derivative step, smoothing polynomial estimated noise-reduced signal.
code: Source of variation            Removed by ─────────────────────────────  ────────────────────────────────────────── Additive baseline              Constant removed by 1st derivative   (dark current, fibre offset) Multiplicative scatter         Linear trend removed by 1st derivative   (particle size, packing,       (equivalent to MSC/SNV at spectral scale)    path-length differences) Electronic noise               Savitzky-Golay smooth pass (2m+1 = 23 pts)   (random spectral noise)        before the derivative step

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: encoder compresses 1 076 (1 701) spectral features 16 latent dimensions. bottleneck forces network discard sensor-specific artefacts reproducible across instruments, retain soil-chemistry variance consistent across training corpus. prediction head reads exclusively latent space, predictions inherently decoupled raw instrument responses.

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: Latent vectors training set define multivariate Gaussian (μ, Σ). new sample squared Mahalanobis distance latent space compared χ² threshold df = 16, α = 0.05 ≈ 26.3: Samples instrument whose spectral profile different anything training set drift far latent space receive --domain flag, even band coverage complete. strictly sensor-aware quality control band selection provide.
code: D²(z) = (z − μ)ᵀ Σ⁻¹ (z − μ)   ✓ in domain if D² ≤ 26.3

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: obtain honest cross-instrument estimate generalisation, cross-validation folds stratified OSSL contributing dataset (random sample). fold holds entire geographic/instrument cluster. resulting mean ± SD metrics (reported ) reflect cross-dataset predictive performance.

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: instrument whose spectral output can resampled canonical OSSL grid compatible. autoSpectra resamples automatically via linear interpolation.

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: Bringing new instrument? Resample spectra model grid (handled automatically predict_soil()), check applicability domain score. Mahalanobis distance tell whether instrument’s spectral profile within training distribution.

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: 10-fold cross-validation stratified OSSL contributing dataset.Format: mean ± SD 10 folds. Metrics updated automatically running train_ossl.R; values literature-representative benchmarks based Safanelli et al. (2023).

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: Metrics 34 OSSL L1 properties generated automatically models/OSSL_VisNIR/metrics_summary.json models/OSSL_MIR/metrics_summary.json running train_ossl.R. app’s Predictions tab displays model-specific R² selected property alongside prediction.

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
code: Input spectrum (d_in preprocessed bands)         │         ▼   ┌──────────────────┐   │   Dense 256 ReLU │   │   Dense 128 ReLU │   │   Dense  64 ReLU │   │   Dense  16 ReLU │ ← Latent space z  (information bottleneck)   └────────┬─────────┘            │     ┌──────┴──────────────────────┐     │                             │     ▼  Reconstruction head        ▼  Prediction head   Dense 32  ReLU               Dense 64  ReLU   Dense d_in  linear           Dropout 5%   ↑ MSE loss (w = 0.3)         Dense 1  linear   (forces encoder to           ↑ MSE loss (w = 0.3)    retain spectral info)       (soil property output)

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
code: Training latent vectors → (μ, Σ)   [16 × 16 covariance]  New sample z:   D²(z) = (z − μ)ᵀ Σ⁻¹ (z − μ)    D² ≤ χ²(16, 0.95) ≈ 26.3  →  ✓ within domain   D² > 26.3                  →  ⚠ out of domain (extrapolation warning)

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: Encoded model_registry pipeline strings:
code: VisNIR (reflectance input)                MIR (absorbance input) ──────────────────────────────────        ────────────────────────────────   Raw reflectance R                         Raw absorbance A           │                                         │           ▼                                         │   A = −ln(R)  [absorbance]                          │   (removes ratio units,                             │    emphasises weak absorptions)                     │           │                                         │           ▼                                         ▼   SG smooth  [ m=11, p=2, d=0, window=23 ]  SG smooth  [ m=11, p=2, d=0, window=23 ]   (reduces electronic noise                 (reduces noise before differentiation)    before differentiation)                          │           │                                         ▼           ▼                               SG 1st deriv [ m=11, p=2, d=1, window=23 ]   SG 1st deriv [ m=11, p=2, d=1 ]        (removes baseline + multiplicative   (removes additive baseline +             scatter between ATR / DRIFTS)    multiplicative scatter)                          │           │                                         ▼           ▼                                 Model input (1 701 features)   Model input (1 076 features) # VisNIR preprocess = c("ABSORBANCE", "SG_SMOOTH(11,2)", "SG_DERIV(11,2,1)")  # MIR preprocess = c("SG_SMOOTH(11,2)", "SG_DERIV(11,2,1)")

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: OSSL v1.2 data publicly available CC-4.0.
code: ossl_download()          # VisNIR + MIR + soillab (~2 GB) ossl_download("visnir")  # VisNIR only ossl_download("mir")     # MIR only

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
code: # Install devtools if needed install.packages("devtools")  # Install autoSpectra devtools::install_github("HugoMachadoRodrigues/autoSpectra")

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: Setting Keras/TensorFlow:
code: install.packages("keras") keras::install_keras()   # installs TensorFlow in a managed virtualenv

what: 1. Download OSSL data and train models
title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: terminal:
code: library(autoSpectra)  # Download OSSL v1.2 (~2 GB, cached locally) ossl_download()  # Train soilVAE for all 34 properties — VisNIR train_ossl_models("OSSL_VisNIR")  # Train soilVAE for all 34 properties — MIR train_ossl_models("OSSL_MIR") Rscript train_ossl.R              # both models Rscript train_ossl.R OSSL_VisNIR  # VisNIR only Rscript train_ossl.R OSSL_MIR     # MIR only

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
code: library(autoSpectra)  # Load your spectra (Soil_ID column + numeric wavelength/wavenumber columns) df <- readxl::read_excel("my_visnir_spectra.xlsx")  # Predict with the OSSL VisNIR model # Works regardless of original instrument — spectra are resampled automatically results <- predict_soil(df, family_id = "OSSL_VisNIR",                         properties = c("oc", "clay.tot", "ph.h2o", "n.tot")) print(results)  # Check applicability domain (Mahalanobis in latent space) app <- predict_applicability(df, "OSSL_VisNIR", "oc") plot_applicability(app)  # Visualize spectra plot_spectra(df, family = get_family("OSSL_VisNIR")) plot_mean_spectrum(df, family = get_family("OSSL_VisNIR"))

what: 3. Pre-load models into memory (recommended for batch jobs)
title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
code: # Load all VisNIR models into session cache (once per session) preload_ossl_models("OSSL_VisNIR")  # Predict many files without disk I/O overhead for (f in my_files) {   df  <- readxl::read_excel(f)   out <- predict_soil(df, "OSSL_VisNIR")   # uses in-memory cache   writexl::write_xlsx(out, sub(".xlsx", "_pred.xlsx", f)) }

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: app provides streamlined four-tab workflow: Model selection first step: choose OSSL VisNIR OSSL MIR sidebar. Models loaded memory first prediction remain cached session. Supported upload formats: .xlsx, .xls, .csv Expected file format: Column headers must wavelength (nm) wavenumber (cm⁻¹) positions. Soil_ID column configurable.

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: OSSL-sensor-agnostic redesign Two official models: OSSL_VisNIR OSSL_MIR — full OSSL v1.2 training, instruments Scientific sensor-agnostic methodology: physics-based SG first-derivative preprocessing + soilVAE information bottleneck + latent applicability domain 10-fold CV stratified OSSL dataset: honest cross-instrument performance estimates (mean ± SD) -memory model cache (R/cache.R): get_cached_model(), preload_ossl_models() — zero-overhead repeated prediction Simplified Shiny UX: two-model selector, lazy preload, applicability domain panel train_ossl.R rewritten: stratified k-fold CV, per-fold metrics, metrics_summary.json R CMD check WARNINGs resolved; pkgdown site live Zenodo DOI registered: 10.5281/zenodo.19004686

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: Major: R package + OSSL integration Converted standalone Shiny app proper R package Added OSSL v1.2 data download integration Expanded 34 OSSL L1 soil properties Two-step SG preprocessing (explicit smooth + derivative passes) MIR support, applicability domain, visualization functions roxygen2 documentation (44 exported functions)

title: autoSpectra — Soil Spectral Modelling
text: Initial release: Shiny application Interactive Shiny app soil spectral prediction 23 soil properties, local training data soilVAE asymmetric autoencoder architecture

what: Apply a spectral preprocessing pipeline — apply_pipeline
title: Apply a spectral preprocessing pipeline — apply_pipeline
text: pipeline defined character vector step names: "ABSORBANCE": convert reflectance → absorbance (−ln R) "SG_SMOOTH(m,p)": Savitzky-Golay smooth (derivative = 0) "SG_DERIV(m,p,d)": Savitzky-Golay derivative (d ≥ 1) "SG(m,p,d)": legacy single-step SG (backward compatible) "SNV": Standard Normal Variate

title: Apply a spectral preprocessing pipeline — apply_pipeline
Apply a spectral preprocessing pipeline — apply_pipeline
text: M Numeric matrix (rows = samples, cols = wavelengths/wavenumbers) steps Character vector preprocessing step strings absorbance_base10 Logical; use base-10 log absorbance conversion

title: Apply a spectral preprocessing pipeline — apply_pipeline
text: Preprocessed matrix

title: Apply a spectral preprocessing pipeline — apply_pipeline
text: recommended two-step pipeline OSSL VisNIR data : c("ABSORBANCE", "SG_SMOOTH(11,2)", "SG_DERIV(11,2,1)") MIR data (already absorbance): c("SG_SMOOTH(11,2)", "SG_DERIV(11,2,1)")

title: Train a soilVAE model for a single soil property — train_soilVAE

title: Train a soilVAE model for a single soil property — train_soilVAE
code: train_soilVAE(   X,   y,   family_id,   prop,   out_dir = "models",   epochs = 30L,   batch_size = 32L,   val_split = 0.2,   patience_es = 8L,   patience_rl = 4L,   latent_dim = 16L,   min_n = 30L )

title: Train a soilVAE model for a single soil property — train_soilVAE
text: X Numeric matrix (rows = samples, cols = preprocessed bands) y Numeric vector soil property values (length == nrow(X)) family_id Family identifier string (used output directory) prop Soil property name (used output file names) out_dir Root output directory (default: "models") epochs Max training epochs (default 30) batch_size Mini-batch size (default 32) val_split Validation fraction training data (default 0.2) patience_es Early stopping patience (default 8) patience_rl LR reduction patience (default 4) latent_dim Latent dimension (default 16) min_n Minimum samples required attempt training (default 30)
